allies.rationale_text: Decomposition supports systems thinking by breaking a complex system into analyzable parts without losing the larger structure.
allies.source_quote: The discipline complements structured problem-solving processes ... The fundamental essence of problem disaggregation uses logic trees to cleave a problem into parts for analysis.

antagonists.rationale_text: Specialization narrows attention to one discipline and undermines the multidisciplinary picture systems thinking depends on.
antagonists.source_quote: Systems thinking counters the natural human inclination for specialization.

antagonists.rationale_text: Cognitive biases like WYSIATI push the thinker toward surface-level stories instead of the wider interacting system.
antagonists.source_quote: Systems thinking requires actively working against this bias by seeking out and incorporating information beyond the surface level.
antagonists.note: The source names WYSIATI directly; the target id uses the existing broader canonical model.

antagonists.rationale_text: Einstellung-effect undermines systems thinking by anchoring attention to the first familiar disciplinary framework — the fixated lens misses the cross-system interconnections that require multi-model synthesis.
antagonists.source_quote: The Law of the Instrument (Maslow's Hammer): Relying exclusively on frameworks derived from a single area of expertise leads to blind spots because they miss the complicated interconnections explained by other models.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Systems-thinking conflicts with simplification when holistic system maps add complexity that obscures the one lever that would actually move the outcome.
structured_tensions.source_quote: systems-thinking conflicts with simplification (simplification) when holistic system maps add complexity that obscures the one lever that would actually move the outcome.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Systems-thinking conflicts with decomposition when reductionist decomposition breaks feedback loops that only exist at the system level.
structured_tensions.source_quote: systems-thinking conflicts with decomposition (decomposition) when reductionist decomposition breaks feedback loops that only exist at the system level.

curation_notes.summary: Systems Thinking has strong first-order ally and antagonist prose, but no explicit structured tension block in the raw file.
donor_drops: Did not import donor tension overlays or compound logic because this pilot is first-order relation semantics only.
open_questions: Law-of-the-instrument / Maslow's Hammer wired in Task 2.5 sweep via einstellung-effect mapping.

allies: The raw ally section is richer than the resolved target-id surface suggests, including multidisciplinary mental models and analogy/metaphor.
antagonists: The raw source explicitly names the law of the instrument, but this pilot avoided forced target-id approximation.
structured_tensions: No explicit structured tension section was preserved from raw source.
